name: spacy-curated-transformers

metadata.name: spacy-curated-transformers
metadata.summary: Curated transformer models for spaCy pipelines

documentation: # 💫 🤖 spaCy Curated Transformers This package provides [spaCy](https://github.com/explosion/spaCy) components and architectures to use a curated set of transformer models via [`curated-transformers`](https://github.com/explosion/curated-transformers) in spaCy. [![PyPi](https://img.shields.io/pypi/v/spacy-curated-transformers.svg?style=flat-square&logo=pypi&logoColor=white)](https://pypi.python.org/pypi/spacy-curated-transformers) [![GitHub](https://img.shields.io/github/release/explosion/spacy-curated-transformers/all.svg?style=flat-square&logo=github)](https://github.com/explosion/spacy-curated-transformers/releases) ## Features - Use pretrained models based on one of the following architectures to power your spaCy pipeline: - ALBERT - BERT - CamemBERT - RoBERTa - XLM-RoBERTa - All the nice features supported by [`spacy-transformers`](https://github.com/explosion/spacy-transformers) such as support for Hugging Face Hub, **multi-task learning**, the extensible config system and out-of-the-box serialization - Deep integration into spaCy, which lays the groundwork for deployment-focused features such as distillation and quantization - Minimal dependencies ## ⏳ Installation Installing the package from pip will automatically install all dependencies. ```bash pip install spacy-curated-transformers ``` ## 🚀 Quickstart An example project is provided in the [`project`](project) directory. ## 📖 Documentation - 📘 [Layers and Model Architectures](https://spacy.io/usage/layers-architectures): Power spaCy components with custom neural networks - 📗 [`CuratedTransformer`](https://spacy.io/api/curatedtransformer): Pipeline component API reference - 📗 [Transformer architectures](https://spacy.io/api/architectures#curated-trf): Architectures and registered functions ## Bug reports and other issues Please use [spaCy's issue tracker](https://github.com/explosion/spaCy/issues) to report a bug, or open a new thread on the [discussion board](https://github.com/explosion/spaCy/discussions) for any other issue.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.hf_loader.build_hf_transformer_encoder_loader_v1
documentation:
Construct a callback that initializes a supported transformer
model with weights from a corresponding HuggingFace model.

name (str):
    Name of the HuggingFace model.
revision (str):
    Name of the model revision/branch.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.remove_eos_bos.remove_bos_eos
documentation: Strips the BOS/EOS piece identifiers from the transformer output.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.with_non_ws_tokens.with_non_ws_tokens
documentation:
Removes non-whitespace tokens from the input before
passing it to the inner model.

documentation: Create an alignment between whitespace and non-whitespace sequences.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.with_non_ws_tokens._filter_tokens
documentation:
Filter out whitespace tokens. Returns the non-whitespace tokens
and a mapping from the (non-whitespace) token offset to the number
of whitespaces that preceded the token.

classes.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.output.TransformerModelOutput
documentation:
Wrapper for PyTorchTransformerOutput consumed by downstream non-PyTorch components.
Also acts as the accumulator for the outputs of subsequent models in the Transformer pipeline.

classes.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.output.DocTransformerOutput
documentation:
Stored on Doc instances. Each Ragged element corresponds to a layer in
original TransformerModelOutput, containing representations of piece identifiers.

documentation:
Construct a model that can be used to convert a list of ragged
piece identifiers to strided spans and vice-versa.

stride (int):
    The stride of the span generator.
window (int):
    The (maximum) size of each span.
batch_size (int):
    The maximum number of spans that are processed
    by the transformer model at a time.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.pytorch.scalar_weight.ScalarWeightClassifier.__init__
documentation:
Construct a scalar weighting module.

```
param n_layers

Number of hidden layers.

param dropout_prob

Dropout applied to the layer weights.

param device

Device on which the module is to be initialized.
```

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.pytorch.scalar_weight.ScalarWeightClassifier.forward
documentation:
Apply scalar weighting to the input.

```
param layer_outputs

Outputs of the hidden layers.

Shape: (batch_size, seq_len, n_layers, width)

returns

Weighted tensor of the layer outputs.

Shape: (batch_size, seq_len, width)
```

Scalar weighting ([Peters et al., 2018]).

[peters et al., 2018]: https://aclanthology.org/N18-1202/
From syntaxdot:
https://github.com/tensordot/syntaxdot/blob/22bd3d43ed2d7fcbef8a6217b01684194fae713f/syntaxdot-transformers/src/scalar_weighting.rs#L62

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.scalar_weight.build_scalar_weight_v1
documentation:
Construct a model that accepts a list of transformer layer
outputs and returns a weighted representation of the same.

num_layers (int):
    Number of transformer hidden layers.
dropout_prob (float):
    Dropout probability.
mixed_precision (bool):
    Use mixed-precision training.
grad_scaler_config (dict):
    Configuration passed to the PyTorch gradient scaler.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.build_transformer_layers_listener_v1
documentation:
Construct a listener layer that communicates with one or more upstream Transformer
components. This layer extracts the output of all transformer layers and performs
pooling over the individual pieces of each Doc token, returning their corresponding
representations.

layers (int):
    The the number of layers produced by the upstream transformer component,
    excluding the embedding layer.
width (int):
    The width of the vectors produced by the upstream transformer component.
pooling (PoolingModelT):
    Model that is used to perform pooling over the piece representations.
upstream (str):
    A string to identify the 'upstream' Transformer component
    to communicate with. The upstream name should either be the wildcard
    string '*', or the name of the Transformer component.

    In almost all cases, the wildcard string will suffice as there'll only be one
    upstream Transformer component. But in certain situations, e.g: you have disjoint
    datasets for certain tasks, or you'd like to use a pre-trained pipeline but a
    downstream task requires its own token representations, you could end up with
    more than one Transformer component in the pipeline.
grad_factor (float):
    Factor to multiply gradients with.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.build_last_transformer_layer_listener_v1
documentation:
Construct a listener layer that communicates with one or more upstream Transformer
components. This layer extracts the output of the last transformer layer and performs
pooling over the individual pieces of each Doc token, returning their corresponding
representations.

width (int):
    The width of the vectors produced by the upstream transformer component.
pooling (Model):
    Model that is used to perform pooling over the piece representations.
upstream (str):
    A string to identify the 'upstream' Transformer component
    to communicate with. The upstream name should either be the wildcard
    string '*', or the name of the Transformer component.

    In almost all cases, the wildcard string will suffice as there'll only be one
    upstream Transformer component. But in certain situations, e.g: you have disjoint
    datasets for certain tasks, or you'd like to use a pre-trained pipeline but a
    downstream task requires its own token representations, you could end up with
    more than one Transformer component in the pipeline.
grad_factor (float):
    Factor to multiply gradients with.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.build_scalar_weighting_listener_v1
documentation:
Construct a listener layer that communicates with one or more upstream Transformer
components. This layer calculates a weighted representation of all transformer layer
outputs and performs pooling over the individual pieces of each Doc token, returning
their corresponding representations.

Requires its upstream Transformer components to return all layer outputs from
their models.

width (int):
    The width of the vectors produced by the upstream transformer component.
weighting (Model):
    Model that is used to perform the weighting of the different layer outputs.
pooling (Model):
    Model that is used to perform pooling over the piece representations.
upstream (str):
    A string to identify the 'upstream' Transformer component
    to communicate with. The upstream name should either be the wildcard
    string '*', or the name of the Transformer component.

    In almost all cases, the wildcard string will suffice as there'll only be one
    upstream Transformer component. But in certain situations, e.g: you have disjoint
    datasets for certain tasks, or you'd like to use a pre-trained pipeline but a
    downstream task requires its own token representations, you could end up with
    more than one Transformer component in the pipeline.
grad_factor (float):
    Factor to multiply gradients with.

classes.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners._ListenerNonPersistentState
documentation: We need to store the listener state in the attributes dict of the listener model.
This custom class is used to ensure that this state is not serialized to disk.

classes.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.ListenerStateUtils

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.ListenerStateUtils.init_state
documentation: Initialize the listener model.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.ListenerStateUtils.use_doc_annotations_for_prediction
documentation:
If True, the listener will perform its operations on the transformer output
annotations stored on the Doc objects. Otherwise, it will perform its operations
on the outputs that were stored directly in it using `TransformerListener.receive`.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.ListenerStateUtils.calculate_batch_id
documentation:
Calculate a content-sensitive hash of the batch of documents, to check
whether the next batch of documents is unexpected.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.ListenerStateUtils.receive
documentation:
Store a batch of training predictions and a backprop callback in the listener.
The predictions and callback are produced by the upstream Transformer component,
and later will be used when the listener's component's model is called.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.ListenerStateUtils.verify_inputs
documentation:
Check that the batch of Doc objects matches the ones we have a
prediction for.

This class provides helper functions to manage the internal state of
`Model`s that act as listeners of the `Transformer` pipe.

classes.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.WrappedTransformerAndListener

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.listeners.WrappedTransformerAndListener.__init__
documentation:
transformer (TransformerModelT):
    Transformer model to wrap.
listener (TransformerListenerModelT):
    Listener model to wrap.
frozen (bool):
    If the transformer is frozen.

Wraps a transformer model and a compatible listener. Exclusively used when replacing
listeners of a shared transformer pipeline.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.make_transformer
documentation:
Construct a CuratedTransformer component, which lets you plug a pre-trained
transformer model into spaCy so you can use it in your pipeline. One or
more subsequent spaCy components can use the transformer outputs as features
in its model, with gradients backpropagated to the single shared weights.

nlp (Language):
    The pipeline.
name (str):
    The component instance name.
model (Model):
    One of the supported pre-trained transformer models.
frozen (bool):
    If `True`, the model's weights are frozen and no backpropagation is performed.
all_layer_outputs (bool):
    If `True`, the model returns the outputs of all the layers. Otherwise, only the
    output of the last layer is returned. This must be set to `True` if any of the pipe's
    downstream listeners require the outputs of all transformer layers.

classes.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.__init__
documentation:
vocab (Vocab):
    The shared vocabulary.
model (Model):
    One of the supported pre-trained transformer models.
name (str):
    The component instance name.
frozen (bool):
    If `True`, the model's weights are frozen and no backpropagation is performed.
all_layer_outputs (bool):
    If `True`, the model returns the outputs of all the layers. Otherwise, only the
    output of the last layer is returned. This must be set to `True` if any of the pipe's
    downstream listeners require the outputs of all transformer layers.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.listeners
documentation:
RETURNS (List[TransformerListenerModelT]):
    The listener models listening to this component. Usually internals.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.listening_components
documentation:
RETURNS (List[str]):
    The downstream components listening to this component. Usually internals.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.add_listener
documentation: Add a listener for a downstream component. Usually internals.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.remove_listener
documentation:
Remove a listener for a downstream component. Usually internals.

RETURNS (bool):
    `True` if successful, `False` otherwise.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.find_listeners
documentation:
Walk over a model of a processing component, looking for layers that
are transformer listeners that have an upstream_name that matches
this component. Listeners can also set their upstream_name attribute to
the wildcard string '*' to match any `Transformer`.
You're unlikely to ever need multiple `Transformer` components, so it's
fine to leave your listeners upstream_name on '*'.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.pipe
documentation:
Apply the pipe to a stream of documents. This usually happens under
the hood when the nlp object is called on a text and all components are
applied to the Doc.

stream (Iterable[Doc]):
    A stream of documents.
batch_size (int):
    The number of documents to buffer.

YIELDS (Doc):
    Processed documents in order.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.predict
documentation:
Apply the pipeline's model to a batch of docs, without modifying them.

docs (Iterable[Doc]):
    The documents to predict.

RETURNS (TransformerModelOutput):
    The extracted features of each document.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.set_annotations
documentation:
Assign the extracted features to the Doc objects. By default, a
DocTransformerOutput object is written to the doc._.trf_data attribute.

docs (Iterable[Doc]):
    The documents to modify.
trf_output (TransformerModelOutput):
    The outputs of the transformer model.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.update
documentation:
Prepare for an update to the transformer.

Like the `Tok2Vec` component, the `Transformer` component is unusual
in that it does not receive "gold standard" annotations to calculate
a weight update. The optimal output of the transformer data is unknown;
it's a hidden layer inside the network that is updated by backpropagating
from output layers.

The `Transformer` component therefore does not perform a weight update
during its own `update` method. Instead, it runs its transformer model
and communicates the output and the backpropagation callback to any
downstream components that have been connected to it via the
transformer listener sublayer. If there are multiple listeners, the last
layer will actually backprop to the transformer and call the optimizer,
while the others simply increment the gradients.

examples (Iterable[Example]):
    A batch of Example objects. Only the `predicted` doc object is used,
    the reference doc is ignored.
drop (float):
    The dropout rate.
sgd (thinc.api.Optimizer):
    The optimizer.
losses (Dict[str, float]):
    Optional record of the loss during training. Updated using the component
    name as the key.

RETURNS (Dict[str, float]):
    The updated losses dictionary.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.get_loss
documentation:
A noop function, for compatibility with the Pipe API. See the `update`
method for an explanation of the loss mechanics of the component.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.initialize
documentation:
Initialize the pipe for training, using data examples if available.

get_examples (Callable[[], Iterable[Example]]):
    Optional function that returns gold-standard Example objects.
nlp (Language):
    The current nlp object.
encoder_loader (Optional[Callable]):
    Initialization callback for the transformer model.
piecer_loader (Optional[Callable]):
    Initialization callback for the input piece encoder.

methods.name: spacy_curated_transformers.pipeline.transformer.CuratedTransformer.finish_update
documentation:
Update parameters using the current parameter gradients.
The Optimizer instance contains the functionality to perform
the stochastic gradient descent.

This method is a noop when the pipe is frozen.

sgd (thinc.api.Optimizer): The optimizer.

DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/pipe#finish_update

spaCy pipeline component that provides access to a pre-trained transformer
model from. Downstream components are connected to this pip using transformer
listener layers. This works similarly to spaCy's Transformer component and
TransformerListener sublayer.

The activations from the transformer are saved in the doc._.trf_data extension
attribute.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tests.models.test_with_strided_spans._add_range
documentation: Add range [0, X.size).

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tests.util.torch_assertclose
documentation: Wrapper around torch.testing.assert_close with custom tolerances.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.architectures.build_albert_transformer_model_v1
documentation:
Construct an ALBERT transformer model.

vocab_size (int):
    Vocabulary size.
with_spans (Callable):
    Callback that constructs a span generator model.
piece_encoder (Model)
    The piece encoder to segment input tokens.
attention_probs_dropout_prob (float):
    Dropout probabilty of the self-attention layers.
embedding_width (int):
    Width of the embedding representations.
hidden_act (str):
    Activation used by the point-wise feed-forward layers.
hidden_dropout_prob (float):
    Dropout probabilty of the point-wise feed-forward and
    embedding layers.
hidden_width (int):
    Width of the final representations.
intermediate_width (int):
    Width of the intermediate projection layer in the
    point-wise feed-forward layer.
layer_norm_eps (float):
    Epsilon for layer normalization.
max_position_embeddings (int):
    Maximum length of position embeddings.
model_max_length (int):
    Maximum length of model inputs.
num_attention_heads (int):
    Number of self-attention heads.
num_hidden_groups (int):
    Number of layer groups whose constituents share parameters.
num_hidden_layers (int):
    Number of hidden layers.
padding_idx (int):
    Index of the padding meta-token.
type_vocab_size (int):
    Type vocabulary size.
torchscript (bool):
    Set to `True` when loading TorchScript models, `False` otherwise.
mixed_precision (bool):
    Use mixed-precision training.
grad_scaler_config (dict):
    Configuration passed to the PyTorch gradient scaler.
wrapped_listener (Optional[TransformerListenerModelT]):
    Optional listener to wrap. Only used when replacing listeners
    in downstream components.

model_max_length (int):

num_attention_heads (int):

num_hidden_groups (int):

num_hidden_layers (int):

padding_idx (int):

type_vocab_size (int):

torchscript (bool):

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.architectures.build_camembert_transformer_model_v2
documentation:
Construct a CamemBERT transformer model.

vocab_size (int):
    Vocabulary size.
with_spans (Callable):
    Callback that constructs a span generator model.
piece_encoder (Model)
    The piece encoder to segment input tokens.
attention_probs_dropout_prob (float):
    Dropout probabilty of the self-attention layers.
dtype (str):
    Data type of the model parameters.
hidden_act (str):
    Activation used by the point-wise feed-forward layers.
hidden_dropout_prob (float):
    Dropout probabilty of the point-wise feed-forward and
    embedding layers.
hidden_width (int):
    Width of the final representations.
intermediate_width (int):
    Width of the intermediate projection layer in the
    point-wise feed-forward layer.
layer_norm_eps (float):
    Epsilon for layer normalization.
max_position_embeddings (int):
    Maximum length of position embeddings.
model_max_length (int):
    Maximum length of model inputs.
num_attention_heads (int):
    Number of self-attention heads.
num_hidden_layers (int):
    Number of hidden layers.
padding_idx (int):
    Index of the padding meta-token.
type_vocab_size (int):
    Type vocabulary size.
torchscript (bool):
    Set to `True` when loading TorchScript models, `False` otherwise.
mixed_precision (bool):
    Use mixed-precision training.
grad_scaler_config (dict):
    Configuration passed to the PyTorch gradient scaler.
wrapped_listener (Optional[TransformerListenerModelT]):
    Optional listener to wrap. Only used when replacing listeners
    in downstream components.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.architectures.build_pytorch_checkpoint_loader_v1
documentation:
Construct a callback that initializes a supported transformer
model with weights from a PyTorch or SafeTensors checkpoint.

path (Path):
    Path to the PyTorch checkpoint.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.architectures.build_pytorch_checkpoint_loader_v2
documentation:
Construct a callback that initializes a supported transformer
model with weights from a PyTorch or SafeTensors checkpoint.

path (Path):
    Path to the directory containing the checkpoint.

classes.name: spacy_curated_transformers.models.architectures._EncoderWrapper
documentation:
Small wrapper to add a prefix that can be used by eg. learning rate
schedules.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tests.test_cli_app.test_fill_config_transformer
documentation: fmt: on

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.util.all_equal
documentation:
Return True if all the elements are equal to each other
(or if the input is an empty sequence), False otherwise.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.util.create_gradual_transformer_unfreezing
documentation:
Construct a callback that can be used to gradually unfreeze the
weights of one or more Transformer components during training. This
can be used to prevent catastrophic forgetting during fine-tuning.

target_pipes (Dict[str, int]):
    A dictionary whose keys and values correspond to the names of Transformer
    components and the training step at which they should be unfrozen respectively.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.bbpe_encoder.build_byte_bpe_encoder_v1
documentation:
Construct a Byte-BPE piece encoder model that accepts a list
of token sequences or documents and returns a corresponding list
of piece identifiers.

This model must be separately initialized using an appropriate
loader.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.bbpe_encoder.build_byte_bpe_encoder_loader_v1
documentation:
Construct a callback that initializes a Byte-BPE piece encoder
model.

vocab_path (Path):
    Path to the vocabulary file.
merges_path (Path):
    Path to the merges file.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.hf_loader.build_hf_piece_encoder_loader_v1
documentation:
Construct a callback that initializes a HuggingFace piece encoder
model. Used in conjunction with the HuggingFace model loader.

name (str):
    Name of the HuggingFace model.
revision (str):
    Name of the model revision/branch.

variables.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.hf_loader.SUPPORTED_TOKENIZERS
documentation: type: ignore

classes.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.hf_loader._HFPieceEncoderLoader
documentation:
This was formerly an inline function. However, only proper objects
can be pickled.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.sentencepiece_adapters.build_xlmr_adapter
documentation:
Align the original fairseq vocab used by pre-trained HF transformer
models with the sentencepiece vocabulary.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.sentencepiece_adapters.build_camembert_adapter
documentation:
Align the original fairseq vocab used by pre-trained Camembert
HF transformer model with the sentencepiece vocabulary.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.char_encoder.build_char_encoder_v1
documentation:
Construct a character piece encoder model that accepts a list
of token sequences or documents and returns a corresponding list
of piece identifiers.

This model must be separately initialized using an appropriate
loader.

normalize (str):
   Unicode normalization to apply before encoding a token. Defaults to
   "NFKC".

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.char_encoder.char_encoder_forward
documentation:
Construct a character piece encoder model that accepts a list
of token sequences or documents and returns a corresponding list
of piece identifiers.

This model must be separately initialized using an appropriate
loader.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.char_encoder.build_char_encoder_loader_v1
documentation:
Construct a callback that initializes a character piece encoder
model.

path (Path):
    Path to the serialized character model.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.sentencepiece_encoder.build_camembert_sentencepiece_encoder_v1
documentation:
Construct a SentencePiece piece encoder model that accepts a list
of token sequences or documents and returns a corresponding list
of piece identifiers with CamemBERT post-processing applied.

This model must be separately initialized using an appropriate
loader.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.sentencepiece_encoder.build_sentencepiece_encoder_v1
documentation:
Construct a SentencePiece piece encoder model that accepts a list
of token sequences or documents and returns a corresponding list
of piece identifiers.

This model must be separately initialized using an appropriate
loader.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.sentencepiece_encoder.build_xlmr_sentencepiece_encoder_v1
documentation:
Construct a SentencePiece piece encoder model that accepts a list
of token sequences or documents and returns a corresponding list
of piece identifiers with XLM-RoBERTa post-processing applied.

This model must be separately initialized using an appropriate
loader.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.sentencepiece_encoder.build_sentencepiece_encoder_loader_v1
documentation:
Construct a callback that initializes a SentencePiece piece encoder
model.

path (Path):
    Path to the serialized SentencePiece model.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.schedules.transformer_discriminative
documentation:
Discriminative learning rate schedule for transformer encoders.

This schedule uses `transformer_schedule` for all transformer encoder
parameters and `default_schedule` for other parameters.

default_schedule (Schedule): default schedule.
transformer_schedule (Schedule): schedule for transformer parameters.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.wordpiece_encoder.build_bert_wordpiece_encoder_v1
documentation:
Construct a WordPiece piece encoder model that accepts a list
of token sequences or documents and returns a corresponding list
of piece identifiers. This encoder also splits each token
on punctuation characters, as expected by most BERT models.

This model must be separately initialized using an appropriate
loader.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.wordpiece_encoder.build_wordpiece_encoder_v1
documentation:
Construct a WordPiece piece encoder model that accepts a list
of token sequences or documents and returns a corresponding list
of piece identifiers.

This model must be separately initialized using an appropriate
loader.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.wordpiece_encoder.build_wordpiece_encoder_loader_v1
documentation:
Construct a callback that initializes a WordPiece piece encoder
model.

path (Path):
    Path to the serialized WordPiece model.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.wordpiece_encoder._bert_preprocess
documentation:
Split a token on punctuation characters. For instance,
'AWO-Mitarbeiter' is split into ['AWO', '-', 'Mitarbeiter']

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.tokenization.wordpiece_encoder._is_bert_punctuation
documentation: Checks whether `char` is a punctuation character.

functions.name: spacy_curated_transformers.cli.debug_pieces.debug_pieces_cli
documentation:
Analyze word- or sentencepiece statistics.
DOCS: https://spacy.io/api/cli#debug-pieces

documentation: type: ignore

variables.name: spacy_curated_transformers._compat.huggingface_hub
documentation: type: ignore

Fill Curated Transformer parameters from HF Hub.
